hct.source: Anemia is a frequent finding in SLE, often due to chronic inflammation and autoimmune hemolysis.

hgb.source: Anemia is a frequent finding in SLE, often due to chronic inflammation and autoimmune hemolysis.

mch.source: Macrocytosis can be seen in SLE, particularly in cases associated with drug-induced hemolysis or vitamin B12 deficiency.

mchc.source: Macrocytosis can be seen in SLE, particularly in cases associated with drug-induced hemolysis or vitamin B12 deficiency.

mcv.source: Macrocytosis can be seen in SLE, particularly in cases associated with drug-induced hemolysis or vitamin B12 deficiency.

plt.source: Thrombocytopenia is a common finding in SLE, resulting from autoimmune destruction of platelets.

rbc.source: Anemia is a frequent finding in SLE, often due to chronic inflammation and autoimmune hemolysis.

rdw.source: Elevated red cell distribution width (RDW) is common in SLE due to increased red cell production variability.

wbc.source: Leukocytosis can occur in SLE due to inflammation and immune complex deposition.
